hookCapture.patchTarget: Parse-postfix (OK: ArgumentExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Parse, OK: OptionExtensions.Parse, OK: Parser.Parse, OK: ParserExtensions.Parse, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParseResultExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: ParserExtensions.Invoke, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: CommandExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParseResultExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: ParserExtensions.InvokeAsync, OK: RootCommand.ctor(String))

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Generates tests from the embedded code snippets in the code documentation.

options.name: input-output
options.description:
Input and output directory pairs containing the *.cs files
The input is separated from the output by the PATH separator (e.g., ';' on Windows, ':' on POSIX).If no output is specified, the --suffix is appended to the input to automatically obtain the output.

options.name: suffix
options.description: Suffix to be automatically appended to the input to obtain the output directory in cases where no explicit output directory was given

options.name: excludes
options.description:
Glob patterns of the files to be excluded from the input.
The exclude patterns are either absolute (e.g., rooted with '/') or relative. In case of relative exclude patterns, they are relative to the _input_ directory and NOT to the current working directory.

options.name: check
options.description:
If set, does not generate any files, but only checks that the content of the test files coincides with what would be generated.
This is particularly useful in continuous integration pipelines if you want to check if all the files have been scanned and correctly generated.

options.name: verbose
options.description: If set, outputs only important messages to the users such as errors
